title: rubus hebecaulis var. parvulidens
side_effects: Rubus hebecaulis var. parvulidens can have side effects if used improperly, such as gastrointestinal issues, including nausea, vomiting, and diarrhea, due to its high tannin content. Prolonged use may also lead to kidney problems, as the plant's active compounds can be toxic to the kidneys. In rare cases, improper use of this plant may cause allergic reactions, such as skin rashes, itching, and swelling. Additionally, consuming large quantities can cause interactions with certain medications, exacerbating underlying health conditions.
